title: Two-pointer and partition scans

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Two-pointer and partition scans; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Two-pointer and partition scans
sources.claim: Two-pointer methods coordinate positions under a monotonic invariant to avoid repeated scanning.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Two-pointer methods coordinate positions under a monotonic invariant to avoid repeated scanning.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: In a sorted pair-sum search, a small sum advances left and a large sum retreats right. Your goal is to recognize, implement and justify a two-pointer sequence solution. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Arrays, strings and matrices; Hash tables, sets and maps; Stacks, queues and deques; Array representation and operations; Correctness and counterexamples. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Two-pointer methods coordinate positions under a monotonic invariant to avoid repeated scanning. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: They often require sorted input or a specific partition property and can fail with negative or nonmonotonic effects. The draft therefore rejects: Two pointers improve every nested-loop array problem to linear time.

includes: Move coordinated indexes under a monotonic or partition invariant.
Recognize, implement and justify a two-pointer sequence solution.

Across C, C++, Java, and Python, keep the state transition and invariant language-neutral first; then map indexes, numeric overflow, recursion, mutable collections, hashing, and library operations to the selected language and retest their guarantees.
excludes: They often require sorted input or a specific partition property and can fail with negative or nonmonotonic effects.

outcomes: Recognize, implement and justify a two-pointer sequence solution.
